Title: Malaysia's prosperity must create opportunities, not just growth
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A country's success cannot be captured by economic figures alone, according to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
The real measure of progress is whether growth translates into better lives - whether people have dignified livelihoods and whether the next generation receives a future filled with greater opportunity.
This is the central purpose of Ekonomi Madani, he said in Khazanah Nasional Bhd's The Khazanah Report 2025 released tonight.
Anwar, who is also Khazanah chairman, said the global economy has become more fragmented and challenging, with shifting supply chains, rapid technological disruption and intensifying strategic competition among major powers reshaping trade, investment and industrial policies.
Countries that fail to adapt risk falling behind, he added.
Malaysia has recognised these challenges and is taking steps to strengthen its economic foundations through initiatives including Ekonomi Madani, the New Industrial Master Plan 2030, the National Energy Transition Roadmap, the National Semiconductor Strategy and the Bumiputera Economic Transformation Plan 2035.
Anwar said these frameworks aim to move Malaysia towards higher-value economic activities and strengthen its position in emerging industries.
However, policies alone will not be enough.
Malaysia needs stronger companies, deeper technical expertise and a greater ability to compete globally.
The country must also be selective in where it focuses its resources, building on real competitive advantages instead of chasing every new trend.
Anwar said the government has an important role, but it cannot carry the transformation alone.
Private investors, entrepreneurs, researchers and workers must also contribute to building a more resilient economy.
This is where Khazanah Nasional plays a role - supporting Malaysia's broader economic ambitions with a long-term perspective.
Anwar said Khazanah's mandate extends beyond short-term financial returns.
It is designed to invest with national priorities in mind, particularly in areas requiring sustained commitment and coordination.
Through initiatives such as Dana Impak, Khazanah aims to strengthen the venture capital ecosystem and help businesses expand into more technology-driven sectors.
The focus areas include semiconductors, advanced manufacturing, digital infrastructure, energy transition and artificial intelligence — industries expected to shape future economic growth and technological capability.
At the same time, economic progress should not come at the cost of national heritage.
Through Dana Warisan and the Warisan KL initiative, Anwar said, Khazanah has supported the restoration of important landmarks including Seri Negara, Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad and Pejabat Pos Besar Lama.
The projects were not merely about preserving old buildings, but about maintaining the identity and character of cities.
"Development should deepen a city's character rather than strip it away."
The restored sites reopened to the public in December 2025 after restoration work began in 2022, reflecting the belief that progress and heritage can move together.
Anwar said Malaysia's challenge now is to remain disciplined and committed in building the capabilities needed to thrive in a more demanding global environment.
The results may not always appear immediately, but long-term investment in people, industries and institutions will determine the country's future resilience.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: EC to decide Negri Sembilan polls dates on June 12
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Election Commission (EC) will hold a special meeting on June 12 to discuss the 16th Negri Sembilan state election.
EC secretary Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus said the meeting, which coincides with the 16th Johor state election special meeting, will be held following the dissolution of the 15th Negri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly last Friday.
He said the meeting would be chaired by EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun who would discuss important dates for the Negri Sembilan state election.
"Among them are the date of the writ of election, nomination day, polling day, electoral roll to be used and other preparations to carry out the election," he said in a statement today.
Khairul Shahril said a press conference would be held immediately after the special meeting.
The 36-seat state assembly was officially dissolved last Friday to pave the way for the election.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gold hits more than two-month low as strong US jobs data boosts rate-hike bets
Article:
NEW YORK: Gold prices hit a more than two-month low on Monday after last week's strong US jobs data boosted expectations of a Federal Reserve rate hike, and as Israel and Iran traded strikes, pushing oil prices higher and fuelling inflation concerns.
Spot gold was down 0.4 per cent at US$4,313.99 per ounce by 1115 GMT, after hitting its lowest level since March 23 earlier in the session. Prices fell by more than three per cent on Friday.
US gold futures for August delivery were down 0.6 per cent at US$4,340.90.
"Spot gold has been sent to a two-month low as markets now expect a Fed rate hike this year following yet another blockbuster US jobs report," said Han Tan, chief market analyst at Bybit.
US nonfarm payrolls increased by 172,000 in May after rising by an upwardly revised 179,000 in April, the US Labour Department's Bureau of Labor Statistics report showed on Friday.
Markets are now pricing in a more than 70 per cent chance of a Fed rate hike in December, up from 45 per cent a week ago, according to the CME FedWatch tool.
While gold is seen as a hedge against inflation, higher interest rates tend to weigh on the non-yielding metal.
Yields on the benchmark 10-year US Treasury note jumped to a two-week high, increasing the opportunity cost of holding gold.
Oil prices rose more than US$4, stoking inflation fears, due to fresh Israeli strikes on Iran as well as renewed attacks on Lebanon a day earlier.
May US consumer price index data on Wednesday and Thursday's producer price report will offer investors further clues on the Fed's monetary policy outlook.
"Gold may next test the psychologically important US$4,000 line for critical support if markets receive hotter-than-expected CPI prints this week, or a decidedly hawkish FOMC next week," Tan said.
Spot silver was down 0.3 per cent at US$67.6 per ounce, platinum lost 0.2 per cent to US$1,772.72, and palladium fell 0.2 per cent to US$1,223.50.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Keyfield buys anchor handling tug supply vessel for RM29.6mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Offshore vessel operator Keyfield International Bhd will buy a 2014-built anchor handling tug supply (AHTS) vessel for US$7.35 million (RM29.6 million).
It is seeking to expand its fleet and capitalise on strong demand for vessel chartering services.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said its unit Keyfield Resolute Sdn Bhd had signed a memorandum of agreement to but the vessel, which will be renamed Keyfield Joyful.
The seller is an unrelated Indonesian company that is part of a Singapore-headquartered company.
Apart from the purchase price, Keyfield expects to invest between RM3 million and RM4 million to progressively upgrade the vessel's specifications.
This includes bollard pull, accommodation capacity and Dynamic Positioning 2 (DP2) capabilities.
In a statement, Keyfield chief executive officer and executive director Darren Kee Chit Huei said the acquisition was a strategic and timely addition.
It will immediately expand the company's AHTS capacity and enable Keyfield to capture near-term chartering opportunities.
"Having recently mobilised three of our existing AHTS vessels — two owned vessels and one third-party vessel managed by the company — to the Middle East, we are actively beefing up our fleet to ensure we can seamlessly meet robust local demand," he said.
Kee added that Keyfield Joyful would allow the company to immediately participate in new tenders and spot charter opportunities without affecting existing commitments.
According to Keyfield, all of its existing AHTS vessels are currently either on charter or earmarked for local and overseas charter contracts.
Meanwhile, two newbuild DP2 AHTS vessels are only expected to be delivered in 2028.
Kee said the medium-term outlook for Malaysia's offshore support vessel sector remains resilient, supported by a widening supply shortage of AHTS vessels with bollard pull capacities below 80 metric tonnes.
"By acquiring Keyfield Joyful, it provides our company with an opportunity to deploy into the tight local market to earn immediate income.
The latest acquisition forms part of Keyfield's disciplined fleet expansion strategy, which is expected to increase its total owned fleet from 14 vessels currently to 18 vessels by 2028.
The expansion pipeline includes three vessels currently under construction, comprising one DP2 accommodation work boat and two DP2 90-tonne AHTS vessels, scheduled for delivery in 2027 and 2028 respectively.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Orange Mitsubishi sparks alarm after reckless driving in Seberang Jaya [WATCH]
Article:
SEBERANG JAYA: An orange Mitsubishi four-wheel drive has sparked public concern after it was allegedly driven recklessly along Jalan Seberang Jaya here, with videos of the incident going viral today.
Videos circulating on social media showed the vehicle moving erratically and repeatedly reversing in the middle of the road.
In another footage, several people were seen attempting to approach the vehicle before taking steps to block its movement to prevent any potential danger to other road users.
Other videos showed the public successfully stopping the four-wheel drive and instructing the driver to get out of the vehicle.
The incident was subsequently shared widely across various social media platforms, with many netizens expressing concern over the driver's actions and calling for a prompt investigation.
The cause of the incident and the identity of the driver remain unknown.
Police have yet to issue a statement on the case.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia issues UMA query to Tanco following share price decline
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Tanco Holdings Bhd has received an unusual market activity (UMA) query from Bursa Malaysia Securities Bhd following a sharp decline in its share price on Monday.
At the close, the stock fell 29.56 per cent, or 47 sen, to RM1.12, with 30.49 million shares transacted.
"In this respect, investors are advised to take note of the company's reply to the UMA query, which will be posted on Bursa Malaysia's website under company announcements, when making their investment decisions," the exchange said in an announcement today.
The exchange said it had sought clarification from the property developer on whether there were any unannounced corporate developments relating to the group's business and affairs, including those under negotiation or discussion that could have contributed to the trading activity.
It also asked whether Tanco was aware of any rumour or report concerning its business and affairs that may account for the trading activity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lorry slams into Kulai toll office after suspected brake failure
Article:
KULAI: A lorry crashed into the Kulai Toll Plaza office, believed to have been caused by brake failure.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Tan Seng Lee said police received information on the crash at 4pm from Expressway Mobile Patrol Vehicle  (EMPV) personnel.
He said preliminary findings showed the 29-year-old lorry driver lost control after reportedly experiencing brake problems before veering to the left side of the expressway and crashing into the toll administration office.
Tan confirmed that no other vehicles were involved in the crash and no toll plaza workers were injured during the incident.
He added that the toll area was operational at the time of the crash, but the impact was confined to the lorry and the structure only.
Police are investigating the case under Section 43(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for careless and inconsiderate driving.
Further investigations are ongoing to determine the exact cause of the accident.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cypark to raise RM52.66mil via private placement for project funding, working capital
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Cypark Resources Bhd has proposed a private placement of up to 10 per cent of its issued share capital involving the issuance of up to 82.28 million new shares to third-party investors, which is expected to raise approximately RM52.66 million.
As at the latest practicable date (LPD) of May 31, 2026, the issued share capital of the company is RM602.13 million, comprising 822.83 million shares, with the company not holding any treasury shares, nor does it have any outstanding convertible securities as at the LPD.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, the integrated renewable energy and environmental solutions company said the amount raised is based on an illustrative issue price of 64 sen per placement share.
Of the total placement proceeds the group plans to utilise RM45 million for the funding of its projects, RM7.07 million for working capital and RM590,000 for the estimated expenses for the private placement.
"The group intends to utilise proceeds of up to RM45 million to fund the group's projects. The proceeds will be utilised to fund, among others, payments to contractors, suppliers and consultants, procurement of materials and equipment, mobilisation costs and other day-to-day operational expenditures incurred in relation to the group's projects," it said.
Among its key ongoing projects are the construction of a 595-megawatt alternating current (MWac) hybrid hydro floating solar photovoltaic plant with battery energy storage systems (BESS) at Terengganu, valued at RM1.96 billion; the Phase 2 development of the SMART waste-to-energy (WtE) plant in Negeri Sembilan, estimated at RM700 million; and a 99.99MWac solar photovoltaic plant in Negeri Sembilan, with an estimated development cost of RM300 million.
It said the exercise was the most appropriate fundraising option as it is comparatively expedient and an efficient avenue to raise the required quantum of funds as opposed to other forms of equity fund raising such as rights issue exercise, where the proceeds are raised on a "lump-sum" basis and such exercise generally takes a longer implementation time.
The proposed private placement also will serve as an additional source of funding for the group without incurring interest cost or having to service principal repayments as compared to if such funding requirements are fully funded via bank borrowings.
"As the proposed private placement may also be implemented in tranches, this enables the company to raise funds expeditiously on an 'as needed' basis, thus minimising full immediate dilution to the existing shareholders' shareholdings.
"Upon completion of the proposed private placement, the enlarged capital base is also expected to strengthen the financial position of the company," it said.
On prospects, Cypark said that it intended to leverage its engineering capabilities to pursue and convert new engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning (EPCC) opportunities, including solar, floating solar and hybrid renewable energy (RE) projects.
"The group is actively pursuing several EPCC tenders to grow its order book, underpinned by a growing execution track record and an increasingly recognised capability.
"As at the LPD, the group's tender book stood at approximately RM2.20 billion, reflecting active participation across large-scale solar, BESS, WtE and other infrastructure opportunities.
TA Securities has been appointed as the principal adviser and placement agent to the company for the proposed private placement.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit ends lower against US dollar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended lower against the US dollar today as stronger-than-expected United States (US) labour market data reinforced expectations that the US Federal Reserve would maintain its restrictive monetary policy stance, boosting demand for the greenback, an economist said.
At 6pm, the local note depreciated to 4.0715/0760 against the US dollar from last Friday's close of 4.0280/0320.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the ringgit eased by 1.04 per cent against the US dollar, in tandem with most Asian currencies, which also traded lower against the greenback.
He said crude oil prices were also higher today due to the ongoing war between the US, Israel, and Iran, which will result in the closure of the Straits of Hormuz.
At the time of writing, the benchmark Brent crude oil increased 3.77 per cent to US$96.60 per barrel.
"A higher inflation rate going forward and how the global market would react to such macroeconomic conditions will be a key factor that will shape market sentiments.
"It appears that a higher benchmark interest rate seems to be the antidote for the currency, and this has led to cautious market sentiments," he told Bernama.
Similar to its performance against the greenback, the ringgit also traded mostly lower against a basket of major currencies.
It depreciated against the British pound to 5.4249/4309 from 5.4233/4287 at the close last Friday, and slid against the Japanese yen to 2.5445/5475 from 2.5183/5209 last week, but turned slightly higher versus the euro at 4.6867/6919  from 4.6882/6928 previously.
The local currency also depreciated against regional peers.
It fell versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1577/1614 from 3.1390/1424 last Friday, and was down against the Thai baht at 12.3942/4128 from 12.3433/3605 previously.
It had also turned weaker against the Philippine peso to 6.60/6.61 from 6.55/6.56 last week, and eased against the Indonesian rupiah to 223.8/224.2 versus 223.3/223.6 previously.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Baby abandoned at surau in JB, CCTV leads probe
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A newborn baby boy was found abandoned inside a tote bag at a surau in Taman Daya here yesterday afternoon.
A 58-year-old man was left distraught after discovering the infant near the surau donation box at about 12pm when he went there to perform prayers.
Johor Baru (Selatan) police chief Assistant Commissioner Raub Selamat confirmed that a report was lodged after the discovery.
He said police are tracking down a man in his 20s who is believed to have abandoned the infant, based on closed-circuit television recordings.
He added that following a medical examination, the newborn was reported to be in stable condition and had no injuries.
The case is being investigated under Section 317 of the Penal Code for child abandonment, which carries a maximum jail term of seven years, a fine, or both upon conviction, he said in a statement today.
He added that efforts to track down the suspect are ongoing and urged anyone with information to contact the district police headquarters hotline at 07-218 2323.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Interpol-backed crackdown hits Thailand-Malaysia piracy network
Article:
BANGKOK: Thai authorities, working with international partners, have cracked down on a Thailand-Malaysia online piracy network accused of illegally streaming copyrighted films.
Authorities raided two locations in Chiang Mai province and arrested a suspect believed to have managed subscriber access to the service on behalf of the platform's principal operator in Malaysia.
Director-General of Thailand's Department of Intellectual Property (DIP) Oramon Sapthaweetham said the operation was carried out under Interpol's Stop Online Piracy (I-SOP) programme with support from the Royal Thai Police, Malaysian police, the Korean National Police Agency (KNPA), and rights holders.
"The operation led to the dismantling of the network, which distributed copyrighted films without authorisation to paying subscribers in Thailand and Malaysia," Oramon told reporters on Monday.
She said the raids were conducted at company premises in Suthep subdistrict and a residence in Mae Hia subdistrict in Chiang Mai province.
"Items seized included accounting documents, payment records linked to cloud-computing service providers, email correspondence, mobile phones, and computers believed to have been used in the operation," she said.
She added that the suspect was charged with joint copyright infringement for commercial purposes, including the unauthorised reproduction, adaptation, and distribution of cinematographic works.
Oramon said the operation reflected Thailand's intensified efforts to combat online piracy and protect the film industry, copyright holders, and the creative economy.
She added that the department would strengthen monitoring and enforcement ahead of the FIFA World Cup to curb illegal livestreaming, the sharing of unauthorised viewing links, and the commercial exploitation of match footage.
Under Thai law, copyright infringement is punishable by fines of between 20,000 baht and 200,000 baht. Offences committed for commercial gain carry penalties of up to four years' imprisonment, fines ranging from 100,000 baht to 800,000 baht, or both.
The department also urged the public not to support copyright-infringing websites and platforms, warning that piracy harms creators, the creative industry, employment, and the wider economy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Queen graces Gathering Light Kuala Lumpur conference
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia graced the inaugural Gathering Light Kuala Lumpur conference at KLCC here yesterday.
According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar's official Facebook page, the conference brought together Muslim leaders, scholars and intellectuals to discuss contemporary issues concerning Islam and the Muslim community.
During the event, Her Majesty attended a discussion on the unity of the ummah in the end times, presented by director of the Al-Ihsan Institute in the United Kingdom and Singapore, Sheikh Dr Ahmad Saad, and preacher Ustaz Engku Ahmad Fadzil. Former president of Yayasan Dakwah Islamiah Malaysia (Yadim), Dr Yusri Mohamad, moderated the session.
[[nid:1458321]]
Founder and former director of the Raja Zarith Sofiah Centre for Advanced Studies on Islam, Science and Civilisation (RZS-CASIS), Dr Wan Mohd Nor Wan Daud, delivered a keynote address titled The Problem of the Loss of Adab.
Prominent preacher and chairman of Yayasan Ar-Ri'ayah, Habib Ali Zainal Abidin Al-Hamid, also delivered a lecture on returning to the path of Allah in an era marked by growing division.
Following the conference, Raja Zarith Sofiah granted an audience to Habib Ali, Sheikh Dr Ahmad and renowned scholar Sheikh Muhamed Hydara Al-Jilani, chairman of the Qadriyya Association of The Gambia.
"I have previously met and attended lectures delivered by all of today's speakers.
"The knowledge and insights shared are valuable guidance for all of us as we navigate an increasingly uncertain world and strive to build a better future for the nation and the ummah," Her Majesty said. —  BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCMC orders immediate improvements to cellular coverage in Kampung Tinagat
Article:
TAWAU: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has directed telecommunications service providers to take immediate measures to improve the quality of communications coverage in Kampung Tinagat here.
In a statement today, MCMC said the directive followed a working visit and assessment of communications coverage and service performance conducted at three identified locations in the village.
The visit, attended by the Kampung Tinagat Village Development and Security Committee (JKKK), follows complaints from Apas assemblyman Datuk Nizam Abu Bakar Titingan and residents over poor cellular service quality.
MCMC said Wireless Broadband Access Service (WBAS) tests found that two of the three locations assessed failed to meet the commission's mandatory standards.
The findings were conveyed to the relevant service providers on May 29, with the companies given seven working days to submit their feedback and action plans.
The commission said notices under Section 51(2) of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 would be issued to any service provider that fails to respond within the stipulated period.
To ensure immediate improvements, CelcomDigi has been instructed to enhance network coverage and submit short- and long-term action plans.
"Maxis has also been directed to carry out maintenance works at the Kampung Tinagat telecommunications tower site," the statement said.
MCMC said it would continue to monitor the implementation of the improvement measures to ensure network quality issues are effectively addressed for the benefit of residents.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Trust, once broken, is hard to rebuild,' says Pas leader on ties with Bersatu
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pas' position within Perikatan Nasional (PN) continues to be defined by questions over trust within the coalition.
Pas information chief Ahmad Fadhli Shaari said while he trusted Bersatu president Tan Sri
Muhyiddin Yassin
's intention to maintain good relations with the party, political cooperation must be measured by actions, not words.
"I do not doubt Tan Sri Muhyiddin's sincerity in expressing that position. However, political relations built on understanding and trust cannot be judged only through positive statements.
"They must also be measured through actions taken during critical moments," he said in a Facebook post today.
Referring to developments in Perlis, Fadhli said questions remained over Bersatu's move to submit a name for the menteri besar post without consulting Pas.
He also raised questions over claims involving a royal decree and the existence of statutory declarations (SDs) linked to assemblymen, saying differing accounts had created further confusion.
He said Pas investigations had confirmed the existence of the SDs based on confessions from
three former Pas assemblymen
, witnessed by party leadership.
On
Negri Sembilan
, he claimed a jointly agreed stance among PN assemblymen had reportedly been changed following a Bersatu political bureau decision without consultation with Pas or other PN leadership.
He said excuses that Pas secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan could not be reached were difficult to comprehend, and questioned why urgent decisions had to be made without prior discussion.
"These two episodes have left a deep impact on the level of trust among partners in Perikatan Nasional.
"Relations can continue. Cooperation can be strengthened. However, trust is a different matter.
"Trust, once broken, is hard to rebuild."
Fadhli added that any decision Pas makes would take into account not only current circumstances but also past events that had eroded trust within the coalition.
Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang was reported as saying that the party will
re-evaluate its cooperation
with Bersatu following several actions by the party that allegedly violate the spirit of camaraderie within PN.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hextar Capital unit wins RM60.4mil dam upgrading job in Perlis
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Hextar Capital Bhd's subsidiary Legacy Core Sdn Bhd has secured a RM60.4 million subcontract from Jati Segera Sdn Bhd for upgrading works at the Timah Tasoh Dam in Perlis.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, Hextar Capital said Legacy Core has accepted a letter of award (LOA) dated June 8, 2026, from Jati Segera.
According to the filing, the project owner is the Department of Irrigation and Drainage (JPS), while Jati Segera is the main contractor.
The contract period runs from June 9, 2026 to June 15, 2027.
"The LOA is expected to contribute positively to the earnings and net assets of Hextar Capital for the financial year ending Dec 31, 2026 and throughout the duration of the contract.
"The LOA will not have any effect on the issued share capital and shareholding structure of the company," it said.
Hextar Capital said none of its directors, major shareholders or persons connected to them has any direct or indirect interest in the contract.
"The board of directors of Hextar Capital, after having considered all aspects of the LOA, believes that the LOA is in the best interest of the company," it said.
In a statement, Hextar Capital managing director Johnson Chang said the award recognises Legacy Core's capabilities and growing presence in the construction sector.
"Whilst we are dedicated to contributing to the country's infrastructure development, we look forward to working closely with the project stakeholders to achieve the successful completion of the project," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Muhibbah bags RM300mil LNG regasification terminal contract in Lumut
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Muhibbah Engineering (M) Bhd has secured a RM300 million engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning (EPCC) contract from Lumut Maritime Terminal Sdn Bhd (LMTSB).
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the construction company said the contract is for the EPCC of LNG Regasification Terminal 3 (RGT3) marine structure and ancillary works, at Lumut Maritime Terminal 2 (LMT2) in Manjung, Perak.
Muhibbah said it received a letter of award from LMTSB on May 15, 2026, with the contract commencing in May 2026 and a completion date of July 1, 2028.
"The contract is expected to contribute positively to the group's earnings and net assets during the contract period for the current and subsequent financial years," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 8, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed lower today, weighed down by a sharp sell-off in US technology stocks and heightened geopolitical tensions following fresh missile strikes by Iran.
The risk-off sentiment prompted investors across the region to trim exposure to equities, resulting in broad-based weakness across Asian markets.
The ringgit weakened against the US dollar, trading at 4.0700.
In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin saw an uptrend, trading at RM259,139, while Solana rose to RM270.
In contrast, Ethereum fell to RM6,796.
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BIMB Investment launches Shariah-compliant small-cap fund
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: BIMB Investment Management Bhd, a subsidiary of Bank Islam Malaysia Bhd, has launched the Shariah-compliant BIMB Opportunities Fund (BOF).
The fund seeks to invest in small-cap companies with growth potential and generate capital gains over the medium to long term.
BIMB Investment said the new fund expands its product offerings and is designed for investors seeking exposure to emerging businesses with strong growth prospects.
BOF will invest at least 70 per cent of its net asset value (NAV) in Shariah-compliant equities and equity-related securities, while maintaining a minimum of one per cent of NAV in cash and Islamic deposits.
Bank Islam group chief business officer for institutional banking Sharifah Sarah Syed Mohamed Tahir said the fund reflects its commitment to enhancing its retail investment offerings and helping investors build sustainable wealth through diversified investment solutions.
"Small-cap equities offer access to emerging growth opportunities that are often under-recognised, while enhancing portfolio resilience through differentiated performance drivers."
Sharifah Sarah said the BOF adopts an actively managed investment strategy that combines top-down and bottom-up approaches.
At the macroeconomic level, the fund evaluates factors such as gross domestic product growth, interest rates, inflation, currency movements and government policies in Malaysia and key global markets.
At the company level, the fund focuses on identifying small-cap businesses with scalable business models, strong growth potential and capable management teams, while ensuring all investments comply with Shariah principles.
The fund is denominated in ringgit and requires a minimum initial investment of RM500, with subsequent investments starting from RM200.
As an open-ended fund, investors can subscribe at any time.
Bank Islam said its wealth management division will continue to play a key role in supporting the group's growth strategy, with the fund available through relationship managers at the bank's branches nationwide.
As of April 30, BIMB Investment managed 17 investment funds and private mandates, including seven Shariah-compliant Sustainable and Responsible Investment funds, with total assets under management of around RM1.7 billion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Israel and Iran must 'immediately stop "shooting"': Trump
Article:
WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump on Monday told Iran and Israel to stop fighting after the two foes attacked each other's territory for the first time since a shaky ceasefire put five weeks of war on hold.
Iran fired dozens of missiles at Israel overnight and Israel responded by targeting military sites in the Islamic republic, sparking fears the escalation could usher in a new full-scale conflict after the April 8 truce.
With Israel's response, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu apparently defied calls by his ally Trump for restraint, against the background of reports of an increasingly testy relationship between the two men.
"Israel and Iran must immediately stop 'shooting.' President DONALD J. TRUMP," the US leader wrote on his Truth Social network.
Minutes later, he added in a new post that "final negotiations" towards peace were proceeding "subject to ignorance or stupidity getting in its way."
Teheran's strikes followed attacks by Israel against targets of the Iran-backed Lebanese Shia group Hizbollah in the southern suburbs of Beirut.
Iran had repeatedly warned it would strike Israel if the Lebanese capital was targeted.
Oil prices surged more than five percent on worries that war could break out again, with hopes now punctured of a rapid end to the standoff that has seen shipping limited through the key Strait of Hormuz trade bottleneck amid fears of global energy and goods shortages.
The strikes also came at a critical moment with diplomatic efforts to end the conflict involving mediator Pakistan on a knife-edge.
Iranian foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei warned at a press conference in Tehran attended by AFP that is was "perfectly natural that the diplomatic process initiated to put an end to this imposed war would be affected."
But he added: "Diplomatic consultations are naturally continuing in all circumstances."
As he was speaking at the foreign ministry, a huge explosion shook the building, followed by repeated explosions believed to be from air defence systems, the AFP reporter said.
Local media in Iran that a "hostile drone" was shot down over Tehran by air defences.
Pakistan Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi visited Tehran to deliver what he said was a "special letter" to Iran's supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei, according to Iranian state television. He has since travelled back to Pakistan, an official Pakistani source said on Monday.
Tehran has insisted any deal to permanently end the war must also halt the parallel conflict in Lebanon, where Israel was pursuing a campaign against Hizbollah.
No casualties have been reported in either Israel or Iran.
The Israeli military said it struck and dismantled Iranian defence systems deployed across several areas in the country. Iran fired nearly 30 missiles towards Israel since Sunday night, an Israeli military official said.
AFP journalists in Jerusalem and the West Bank city of Ramallah heard a series of explosions and the Israeli army said it worked to intercept a new wave of Iranian missiles.
Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they had struck Israel's Nevatim and Tel Nof air bases and had also targeted a petrochemical facility in Israel in retaliation for an attack on a similar site in southwestern Iran.
The Guards warned that Israel "has initiated a dangerous game, the scope of which will encompass all energy-related targets in the region".
A military source told the Tasnim news agency that "Iran is prepared for a long-term war with the Zionist regime and for strikes against US interests" in the region.
Yemen's Iran-backed Houthi rebels meanwhile announced a missile attack on Israel on Monday, the first since early April, and declared a ban on Israeli shipping in the Red Sea.
The European Union's top diplomat Kaja Kallas called on both sides to "sit down to a negotiation table and agree", adding that "the region does not need an escalation."
It also remains unclear who is leading decision-making in Teheran with Mojtaba Khamenei, said to have been wounded in a US-Israeli strike, yet to appear in public after taking over from his father Ali Khamenei who was killed on the first day of the war on February 28.
On Monday in Tehran, there was little sign of any return to war. Under sunny skies, cafe terraces were packed, while motorcycles weaved at high speed through the lunchtime traffic, an AFP correspondent in the capital said.
But the traffic seemed lighter than usual for a weekday, suggesting that some people had stayed home and there were also many more people queueing at gas stations.
In an interview with the Financial Times on Sunday, referring to Netanyahu, Trump insisted he held sway in their relationship.
"I call the shots. I call all the shots. He doesn't call the shots," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AmBank to buy Menara AmBank in RM331mil deal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: AMMB Holdings Bhd (AmBank) plans to buy Menara AmBank here from AmFIRST Real Estate Investment Trust (AmFIRST Reit) for RM331 million.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, the banking group said its subsidiary AmBank (M) Bhd had signed a conditional sale and purchase agreement with Maybank Trustees Bhd, acting as trustee for AmFIRST Reit, to buy the 46-storey office tower together with related assets and rights.
The acquisition includes all tenancies, licences, operational assets and essential contracts related to the building's operation and maintenance.
Menara AmBank, located in the Golden Triangle, is a freehold commercial office building with a net lettable area of 453,419 square feet.
The property currently has an occupancy rate of 77.8 per cent.
AmBank said it is the anchor tenant of the building, occupying 65.6 per cent of the total net lettable area.
"The proposed acquisition is an opportunity for AmBank to own the freehold building in which its offices are located.
"Ownership of Menara AmBank allows AmBank to secure its long-term office tenure and potentially mitigate increases in occupancy-related costs," the company said.
The purchase price was determined on a willing buyer-willing seller basis after considering an independent valuation of RM333 million conducted by PPC International Sdn Bhd.
The valuation was based primarily on the income capitalisation method, reflecting the property's status as an income-generating asset.
For the financial year ended March 31, 2025, Menara AmBank generated rental income of about RM19.9 million and net property income of RM9.4 million.
AmFIRST Reit acquired the property in December 2006 for RM230.17 million.
AmBank will fund the acquisition entirely through internally generated funds.
Upon signing the agreement, the bank paid a seven per cent deposit amounting to RM23.17 million and will also pay a real property gains tax retention sum of RM9.93 million.
The proposed acquisition remains subject to approval from AmFIRST Reit unitholders at an extraordinary general meeting and confirmation or approval from the Economy Ministry.
Barring unforeseen circumstances, the transaction is expected to be completed in the fourth quarter of 2026.
AmBank said the acquisition is not expected to have any material effect on the company's net assets, earnings per share or gearing for the financial year ending March 31, 2027.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sarawak twins gain new experiences through NS training in Pahang
Article:
PEKAN: Being selected for the National Service Training Programme (PLKN) 3.0 gave Sarawak twins Alfred Juan Ellina and Albert Noel the opportunity to travel to Peninsular Malaysia.
Juan and Noel, both 18, from Bintulu, Sarawak, described their 45-day stay at the 505th Territorial Army Regiment Camp here as an eye-opener, allowing them to mingle with people of different races, hear and speak various dialects, and experience a wide range of local food.
Juan said the programme also gave them the opportunity to travel to the east coast state, and they plan to return in the future.
"Both of us were selected and decided to take up the offer to explore new places despite the distance. We are eager to return to Sarawak tomorrow as we have not met our families for more than 45 days.
"The programme was fun, especially being surrounded by new friends. Most of us enjoyed handling firearms and shooting practice," said Juan, who aspires to become a lawyer.
Meanwhile, Noel, who hopes to become a teacher, said that upon returning to Bintulu, both of them plan to continue their studies in Form Six.
"We were fortunate to be offered the programme at the same camp. Although we studied in different classes at school, we prefer to be together," he said.
The duo were among 531 male trainees who completed their 45-day training at the camp today.
National Service Training Department director-general Major-General Datuk Marzuki Mokhtar was present at the passing-out parade.
Meanwhile, Umar Muhaimin Mustafar Kamal, 18, who was named Wira Muda Ulung at the camp, said the programme modules had a positive impact on character development and fostering national unity.
The Kuantan-born trainee said his time at the camp allowed him to mingle with peers from various backgrounds, with group activities requiring them to face challenges together.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Businesses invited to champion environmental excellence
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR:The Prime Minister's Hibiscus Award committee 2026/2027 is calling on businesses to take part in one of Malaysia's longest-running platforms recognising outstanding environmental management practices.
The opening of the 2026/2027 cycle comes at a time when strong environmental management is becoming crucial to business resilience, market access and long-term competitiveness.
Federation of Malaysian Manufacturing president Jacob Lee Chor Kok said environmental management can no longer be viewed as a standalone or performative exercise.
"Businesses today are expected to demonstrate robust governance systems, clearer accountability and measurable progress in identifying, managing and reducing environmental impact across their operations and value chains.
"What makes PMHA important is that it focuses on good practices and continuous improvement.
"The assessment process allows organisations to identify gaps, benchmark progress and strengthen the maturity of their practices over time, while encouraging a more structured and results-oriented approach to environmental management," said Lee, who spoke as chairman of the award's organising committee.
The just concluded 2024/2025 PMHA cycle showed encouraging progress across participating organisations.
More companies had formal environmental management systems in place, introducing clearer carbon reduction initiatives and integrating environmental considerations more meaningfully into operational and strategic decision-making.
However, the broader reality is that environmental management maturity across Malaysian businesses remains uneven, particularly beyond larger corporates.
"This is why broader participation matters. Strong environmental management can no longer be viewed as relevant only to large organisations or traditionally high-impact industries.
"As market expectations, supply chain requirements and regulatory scrutiny continue to evolve, businesses of all sizes will increasingly be expected to demonstrate more structured,  measurable and credible environmental practices.
"We hope to see stronger SME participation in this coming cycle as SMEs are an integral part of Malaysia's industrial and supply chain ecosystem," said Lee.
The PMHA 2026/2027 cycle is open to organisations across all sectors - manufacturing, oil and gas, services, financial, food and beverages and hotels including both large enterprises and small and medium enterprises.
Participants will undergo a two-stage assessment process comprising written submissions and on-site evaluations covering areas.
This includes environmental governance, compliance, operational management and carbon footprint management.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mum arrested after 8-year-old girl dies, brother critically injured in 12th-floor apartment fall
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Police arrested a 37-year-old Indonesian woman following the death of her eight-year-old daughter and serious injuries suffered by her 10-year-old son after both children fell from the 12th floor of an apartment building in Tampoi here yesterday.
Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said police received a report on the incident at 8.27am after the two children were found to have fallen from the apartment block.
The girl was
pronounced dead
at the scene, while her brother sustained severe injuries and is receiving treatment at Sultanah Aminah Hospital.
Acting on information police arrested the children's mother at noon on the same day to facilitate investigations.
Rahaman said the suspect has no previous criminal record and tested negative for drugs following a urine screening.
"The suspect was brought before the magistrate's court today for a remand order to assist investigations," he said in a statement.
Several witnesses who were at the scene and allegedly saw the children fall from the 12th floor have also been called in to have their statements recorded.
Police are investigating the case under Section 31(1)(a) of the Child Act 2001 for alleged child neglect, abuse, abandonment or exposure. If convicted, an offender may be fined up to RM50,000, jailed for up to 20 years, or both.
Rahaman said investigators have yet to determine the actual cause and motive behind the incident and believe the arrest of the children's mother could help establish the circumstances that led to the tragedy.
Members of the public with information related to the case have been urged to contact the Johor police hotline at 019-2792095 or the Johor police contingent operations room at 07-2212999.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fire destroys 15 houses in Kota Baru
Article:
KOTA BARU: The number of houses destroyed in a fire at Jalan Kampung Cina here today has risen from 10 to 15.
Firemen also managed to save 10 nearby houses from suffering the same fate.
Kelantan Fire and Rescue Department Zone 1 chief Khairul Ariffin Che Ramli said the department received a distress call at
12.34pm
before the first fire engine from the Kota Baru Fire and Rescue Station arrived at the scene five minutes later.
He said the blaze was brought under control at 12.46pm.
"The operation involved four fire engines, a water tanker, 38 fire personnel, as well as five volunteer firemen.
"Water was sourced from nearby fire hydrants and the Kelantan River to ensure sufficient supply throughout the operation," he told reporters at the scene.
He said the houses, most of which were wooden structures built close to each other, caused the flames to spread rapidly.
"The hot weather at the time of the incident also contributed to the fire spreading quickly from one house to another," he said.
He said the number of affected families was still being compiled, while the cause of the fire and the estimated losses had yet to be determined.
"No casualties have been reported in the incident," he added.
Meanwhile, one of the victims, Rohani Ab Ghani, 55, said she immediately alerted her neighbours after noticing the fire and urged them to move their belongings out of their homes.
"I shouted to my neighbours and told them to save whatever belongings they could before the flames spread further.
"I am grateful the firefighters arrived quickly and managed to stop the fire from spreading to my house, which is located about 10m from the house that was engulfed in flames," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: When one truck slows, traffic stops
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Heavy vehicles often cause congestion on steep highway gradients, reducing multi-lane roads to a single lane highway.
Experts noted that these occurrences are "well-known" traffic problem as highway flow will collapse behind the slow moving trucks.
"Once overtaking starts between slow-moving heavy vehicles, congestion waves can ripple backwards for kilometres.
"This becomes particularly severe during festive periods when traffic volumes spike sharply across the national expressway network," MY Mobility Vision founder and senior adviser Wan Agyl Wan Hassan told Business Times.
In the short term, Wan Agyl said several practical measures can help to ease congestion.
He suggested a dedicated climbing lanes for heavy vehicles or a freight relief line.
Wan Agyl also said a stricter enforcement on truck overtaking behaviour as well as management of overloaded lorries could curb the problem.
"Overloaded trucks are a major issue because they not only damage roads faster, but also move slower on inclines and require longer braking distances, worsening congestion and safety risks simultaneously.
"In the longer term, Malaysia must reduce excessive dependence on highways for long-haul freight movement. Without stronger rail freight alternatives and better logistics planning, congestion pressure will continue rising regardless of how many additional lanes are built," he added.
Meanwhile, transport economist Dr Rosli Azad Khan suggested a switch of the cargo journeys to rail, especially for journeys more than 200km.
"The cargo shippers should demand for more rail capacity, higher frequency service and cheaper rates for them to switch to rail.
Train journeys should be cheaper provided rail operators can also offer reasonable lifting rates and efficiency services from rail to lorries for the last mile delivery," he said.
He also said more cargo villages should be established in the country , mainly places where cargoes are brought to the rail sidings, get lifted onto rails and transported to the various destinations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar's Japan visit to see signing of four strategic MoUs
Article:
TOKYO: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's official visit starting today is set to elevate Malaysia-Japan ties with the signing of four strategic memorandums of understanding (MoUs).
Malaysian Ambassador to Japan Datuk Shahril Effendi Abd Ghany said the invitation reflects Tokyo's high regard for Malaysia, with Anwar receiving an official welcome at the Japanese Prime Minister's Office.
Anwar will lead the Malaysian delegation in a bilateral meeting with his Japanese counterpart, Sanae Takaichi.
"Both leaders are also scheduled to hold a joint press conference.
"The prime minister is expected to make an announcement on the signing of memoranda of understanding and letters of intent between Malaysia and Japan in regional defence and security, healthcare, energy transition, environmental cooperation and higher education.
"Both leaders will hold a joint press conference where the prime minister will announce the MoUs and letters of intent before attending an official luncheon hosted by Takaichi," Shahril told a press conference ahead of the visit at the Malaysian Embassy here today.
Anwar's arrival at Haneda International Airport at 8.05pm Japan time (7.05pm in Malaysia) will be accompanied by Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Minister Steven Sim, and senior officials.
Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan did not join the delegation.
Shahril noted that the visit underscores Malaysia's role as a key partner to Japan in Asean amid geopolitical uncertainties and energy security challenges.
"Japan views Asean as a vital strategic ally. Malaysia is uniquely positioned to leverage this, and we have assured Tokyo of our readiness to cooperate closely.
"Japan highly values the opportunity to expand ties beyond Kuala Lumpur, as Asean as a whole is a highly important strategic partner for them. They understand the region's importance and view Asean as a key partner in various areas of cooperation," he said.
The visit comes ahead of next year's 70th anniversary of Malaysia-Japan diplomatic ties, established since Malaysia's independence in 1957.
"It is crucial that we continue to strengthen the bilateral bond built over these seven decades," he added.
Bilateral trade stood at RM142.96 billion in 2025. As of December 2025, Japan has participated in 2,872 manufacturing projects in Malaysia, valued at RM107.9 billion and creating 347,346 jobs, Wisma Putra said in a statement.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia secures RM92.8bil investments in Q1, Japan leads foreign inflows
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia continues to attract investor confidence despite global uncertainty, securing RM92.8 billion in approved investments in the first quarter (Q1) of 2026 with Japan emerging as the largest foreign investor.
The approved investments - spanning 1,249 projects across the services, manufacturing and primary sectors - are expected to create 50,226 jobs.
This was a 46.7 per cent increase from the same period last year, highlighting stronger employment impact from new investments, according to the Malaysia Investment Developtment Authority (MIDA).
Although total approved investments were marginally lower than RM93 billion in Q1 2025, the latest figures underline Malaysia's resilience amid geopolitical tensions and shifting global economic conditions.
MIDA chairman Tengku Datuk Seri Utama Zafrul Abdul Aziz said the performance reflected the country's growing ability to attract technology-driven and high-value investments.
"Malaysia's Q1 2026 investment performance sends a clear signal that our economic fundamentals remain strong. Global and domestic investors continue to place confidence in this nation," he said.
Japan topped foreign investment sources with RM21.5 billion, a sharp increase from RM1.6 billion a year earlier. This was followed by China and the United States with RM10.1 billion each, Singapore (RM6.7 billion) and Thailand (RM2.5 billion).
MIDA said 93.6 per cent of Japanese investments approved during the quarter were channelled into digital transformation activities, reinforcing Malaysia's position in high-technology and digital supply chains.
Selangor led approved investments with RM33.5 billion, followed by Johor and Kuala Lumpur at RM16.9 billion each, Penang (RM6.2 billion) and Sarawak (RM4 billion).
The services sector remained the key investment driver, accounting for RM60.8 billion or 65.5 per cent of total approvals.
Information and communications led the segment with RM38.9 billion, driven by demand for artificial intelligence (AI), cloud computing and data centres.
Data centre and cloud investments alone accounted for RM34.6 billion across 33 projects, reflecting Malaysia's growing role as a regional digital infrastructure hub.
The manufacturing sector attracted RM24.1 billion across 501 projects, with electrical and electronics, chemicals, machinery and equipment among the top contributors.
Despite a lower investment value compared with last year due to a large one-off project in Q1 2025, the number of approved manufacturing projects rose 142 per cent.
The sector also remained the largest job creator, generating 30,468 expected jobs, with 17.9 per cent offering salaries above RM5,000 monthly.
Meanwhile, the primary sector recorded RM7.9 billion in approved investments, driven by oil and gas projects, particularly in Sarawak.
MIDA chief executive officer Datuk Sikh Shamsul Ibrahim Sikh Abdul Majid said Malaysia's strong implementation track record remained a key attraction for investors.
Between 2021 and February this year, 85 per cent of approved manufacturing projects had reached various implementation stages, reflecting confidence in Malaysia's investment ecosystem.
MIDA is currently facilitating RM38.3 billion worth of potential projects, while discussions are ongoing for another RM91 billion in possible investments.
The agency said Malaysia remains well-positioned for long-term growth, supported by strong pipelines in semiconductors, AI infrastructure, renewable energy and advanced medical technology.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MMA: Ensure enough staff before building PJ hospital
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Medical Association (MMA) has urged the Health Ministry to ensure sufficient medical personnel are in place before work begins on the proposed public hospital in Petaling Jaya.
MMA president Dr R. Thirunavukarasu said Malaysia faces a severe workforce deficit, with a shortage of nearly 11,000 specialists.
He said the nursing vacancy rate of about 18 per cent represents 14,700 unfilled posts out of 84,000 nationwide.
"Furthermore, out of 5,000 medical graduate positions offered recently, only 529 were taken up.
"Our hospitals are not short of demand. They are short of staff," he said in a statement.
Thirunavukarasu said there should not be a repeat of what happened at Pasir Gudang Hospital, which was completed and handed over but could not open fully due to insufficient doctors and nurses to run it safely.
"That is not a failure of construction. It is a failure of workforce planning."
He said the ministry could maximise existing private healthcare infrastructure rather than relying solely on new physical builds, noting that Petaling Jaya alone houses 12 private hospitals with more than 1,400 beds and over 450 registered private clinics.
"MMA urged the ministry to expand its existing outsourcing mechanisms, with nearly 100 private hospitals shortlisted to manage public patients for specialised treatments, including cardiology, nephrology and radiology imaging," he said.
He also called for stronger financial support for Malaysia's healthcare digitalisation plans managed by the ministry's Digital Health Division.
He said MMA maintained its call for the establishment of an independent Health Service Commission to separate healthcare human resources from standard civil service structures, ensuring doctors are no longer constrained by systemic issues.
It was reported that the proposed Petaling Jaya Hospital will be constructed as a vertical facility with a capacity of
500
beds.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dzulkefly: Suicide cases remain alarmingly high despite slight drop in 2025
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia continues to face a serious challenge in addressing suicide, with 1,079 cases recorded last year despite a slight decline from the previous year, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said.
Citing police data, Dr Dzulkefly said suicide cases had generally trended upwards over recent years before recording a modest decrease in 2025.
"A total of 981 cases were recorded in 2022 before rising to 1,087 in 2023, an increase of 10.8 per cent.
"In 2024, the figure climbed further to 1,114 cases, representing a 2.48 per cent increase from the previous year.
"Although the number fell to 1,079 in 2025, it remains at a worrying and unacceptably high level," he said.
He was speaking at the Roundtable Discussion on Safe and Responsible Media Reporting of Suicidal Behaviour at the Putrajaya International Convention Centre (PICC) today.
The session, which brought together Dzulkefly and Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil with media practitioners, was held under the theme "Media's Role in Preventing Suicidal Behaviour: Changing Perceptions, Building Hope".
The event also featured the signing of the Safe and Responsible Media Reporting Pledge on Suicidal Behaviour. Also present was Communications and Multimedia Content Forum of Malaysia (CMCF) chairman Datuk Rafiq Razali.
Dzulkefly said the Health Ministry and Communications Ministry remained committed to strengthening strategic cooperation to address suicide through safe and responsible media reporting practices.
"Suicide can no longer be viewed solely as an individual problem. It must be addressed as a societal, national and humanitarian issue requiring a comprehensive, cross-sectoral approach involving multiple ministries and stakeholders," he said.
He also said that, according to the World Health Organisation, more than 703,000 people worldwide die by suicide each year, equivalent to one death every 40 seconds.
Dzulkefly said each suicide death had a profound impact on at least 135 people close to the individual, including family members and friends.
"Therefore, suicide must be regarded as a societal issue, a national issue and a humanitarian issue that requires collective action," he said.
To address the problem, he said the government would continue strengthening prevention efforts through the National Mental Health Strategic Plan 2020-2026, which includes a dedicated suicide prevention strategy.
He said the ministry launched the Malaysia Suicide Awareness Voice of Hope (MySave) programme in 2023, encompassing prevention, early intervention, treatment and ongoing community support initiatives.
"The ministry also operates the Heal 15555 helpline, which provides crisis support services delivered by registered psychologists," he said.
Dzulkefly also highlighted a major policy shift following the repeal of Section 309 of the Penal Code, which took effect on Sept 10, 2025, meaning attempted suicide is no longer treated as a criminal offence.
"The move transformed the country's approach from one centred on punishment to one that is safer, more empathetic and more effective, supported by the role of Crisis Intervention Officers on the front line," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Petronas, Eni establish Searah JV to strengthen regional energy security
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's national oil and gas company
Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) and Italian oil company Eni have announced the formation of Searah, a 50:50 joint venture (JV) that combines selected assets in Malaysia and Indonesia.
In a statement today, Petronas said the JV is a part of a partnership-led approach to strengthen portfolio value, support sustainable energy development and strengthen Malaysia and regional energy security.
"Designed to operate as an independent entity, Searah provides a more focused platform to enhance operational performance and accelerate value creation, in line with Petronas' ongoing efforts to optimise its portfolio and pursue new avenues for growth.
"By combining selected assets across Malaysia and Indonesia, Petronas and Eni have formed synergies in portfolios, technical expertise, and regional insights. This approach enables greater scale, sharper execution and stronger value delivery," said the statement.
Petronas president and group chief executive officer, Tan Sri Tengku Muhammad Taufik Tengku Aziz, said Searah aligns with the company's focus on exercising greater discipline in developing resources, coupled with more agile capital deployment, as well as a stronger emphasis on sustained value creation across the gas value chain.
"Searah is envisaged to bring the operational depth, financial resilience and growth capacity of both partners in addressing the region's growing energy needs reliably and responsibly, even as it contributes towards the long-term security of supply in Indonesia and Malaysia," he said.
Eni chief executive officer Claudio Descalzi said Searah embodies the proven strategy focused on creating high-quality businesses that integrate scale, efficiency and growth.
This is driven by Eni's excellence in exploration and project execution, and the company's continued focus on technology and innovation.
"Searah is a strong new entity in Southeast Asia, the first and largest of its kind in the region,  combining our expertise with Petronas to support the development of energy resources in Indonesia and Malaysia, with a strong commitment to environmental protection and local growth," he said.
The establishment builds on the memorandum of understanding announced in February 2025 and the subsequent signing of the investment agreement announced on Nov 3, 2025, with all required regulatory, governmental and partner approvals obtained.
With a portfolio of 19 gas-producing and development assets — 14 in Indonesia and five in Malaysia — Searah starts with an initial production base of approximately 300,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day (boe/d), targeting over 500,000 boe/d of sustainable production within the next three years.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AirBorneo says flight disruptions to continue as concurrent aircraft maintenance hits Sabah, Sarawak services
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — AirBorneo has confirmed ongoing flight disruptions across Sabah and Sarawak, with delays and cancellations expected to continue over the coming days due to concurrent aircraft maintenance and technical rectification works.
In a statement, the airline said the disruptions began on June 5 and have affected a number of its services in the region.
It said the situation was caused by a combination of unscheduled technical rectification on several aircraft, scheduled maintenance already underway, and operational and crew duty requirements.
“In every case, our decisions have been guided by a single priority: the safety of our passengers and crew,” the airline said.
AirBorneo said aircraft requiring technical attention had been withdrawn from service as a precaution until engineers were fully satisfied they were fit to fly.
It added that with several aircraft undergoing maintenance and technical checks at the same time, its operating fleet had been temporarily constrained.
“As a result, passengers should anticipate the possibility of further schedule changes, cancellations and delays over the coming days while our aircraft are progressively returned to service,” it said.
The airline urged passengers to check their flight status before heading to the airport, adding that its teams were working around the clock to minimise disruption and re-accommodate affected travellers on the earliest available flights.
Affected passengers are being contacted directly and may also reach AirBorneo’s Customer Care team at +60-8253 7555 (international) or 1-300-22-1388 (local), or via its website for assistance and updates.
“We thank our passengers for their patience and understanding and remain committed to delivering safe and reliable connectivity across the region,” it said.

Title: Anwar arrives in Tokyo for three-day Japan visit, Nikkei Forum on agenda
Article:
TOKYO, June 8 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim arrived here tonight for a three-day official visit to Japan, aimed at further strengthening bilateral ties and participating in the 31st Nikkei Forum.
The aircraft carrying the Prime Minister touched down at Haneda International Airport at 8.20pm local time (7.20pm Malaysia time).
Upon arrival, Anwar was received by Malaysia’s Ambassador to Japan Datuk Shahril Effendi Abd Ghany, Japanese Parliamentary Vice-Minister of Foreign Affairs Tomoaki Shimada, Chief of Protocol Tadayuki Miyashita and Ambassador of Japan to Malaysia Noriyuki Shikata.
The visit is Anwar’s first official trip to Japan since assuming office as Malaysia’s 10th Prime Minister in November 2022, although he previously made two working visits in 2023 and 2024.
The Prime Minister’s busy itinerary begins on Tuesday at the University of Tokyo, where he is scheduled to deliver a special lecture titled “Humanity in a Human-Machine Civilisation”.
Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, will later join a roundtable with 29 selected Japanese business leaders and hold meetings with several Japanese companies, including one-on-one sessions with representatives from six firms.
On Wednesday, the Prime Minister will attend the 31st Nikkei Forum, themed “Working Together for a More Resilient and Prosperous Asia”, where he is expected to deliver a keynote address titled “Navigating Strategic Uncertainty”.
Later that day, Anwar will be accorded an official welcoming ceremony at the Office of the Prime Minister of Japan, followed by a bilateral meeting with his Japanese counterpart, Sanae Takaichi.
The visit is expected to yield outcomes including four Memoranda of Cooperation and two Letters of Intent covering defence and regional security, healthcare, energy transition, environmental cooperation and education, as well as a Joint Statement under the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership framework.
Anwar is accompanied on the visit by Minister of Investment, Trade and Industry Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, Minister of Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Steven Sim Chee Keong and senior government officials.
The Prime Minister and his delegation are scheduled to depart for Malaysia on Wednesday afternoon. — Bernama

Title: MACC records 1,095 charges, 563 convictions since 2024; new chief pushes tech-driven crackdown, warns: ‘No more old methods’
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 8 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has recorded 1,095 charges and secured 563 convictions from 2,633 investigation papers opened since 2024, said Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman.
He said 583 of the investigation papers involved high-profile and public interest cases, reflecting the commission’s commitment to taking action regardless of rank or position.
Abd Halim said the MACC’s focus goes beyond prosecuting offenders, with equal emphasis placed on dismantling corruption networks through aggressive asset recovery measures.
“Based on records from the Legal and Prosecution Division up to April 2026, a total of 115 asset forfeiture applications were filed in court, compared with 179 applications for the whole of last year.
“During the period, the MACC seized assets worth RM16.49 million, froze assets amounting to RM425,464.60 and secured forfeitures totalling RM3.42 million. Compounds amounting to RM10.47 million were also collected,” he said in his inaugural address at the MACC Monthly Assembly here today.
Abd Halim stressed that he would not allow the people’s rights to be eroded by those driven by greed.
He said the MACC is also strengthening the capabilities of its officers to tackle increasingly complex financial crimes involving advanced technology, cross-border transactions and cryptocurrencies.
“We can no longer work using old methods. The use of advanced technology, stronger strategic intelligence, data analytics and cooperation with international agencies is essential to ensure we remain one step ahead of criminals,” he said.
Nearly a month into his tenure, Abd Halim introduced five key leadership principles: uncompromising integrity, fair enforcement, prevention over punishment, technology and transparency, and building public trust.
“My personal principle is simple: ‘If you dare to do it, dare to take responsibility for it.’ No one in this organisation should become a liability that tarnishes the image of the MACC, which is currently at its strongest,” he said. — Bernama

Title: EC: Special meeting on N. Sembilan state election on June 12
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — The Election Commission (EC) will hold a special meeting on June 12 to discuss the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election.
EC secretary Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus said the meeting, which coincides with the 16th Johor state election special meeting, will be held following the dissolution of the 15th Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly last Friday.
He said the meeting would be chaired by EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun who would discuss important dates for the Negeri Sembilan state election.
“Among them are the date of the writ of election, nomination day, polling day, electoral roll to be used and other preparations to carry out the election,” he said in a statement today.
Khairul Shahril said a press conference would be held immediately after the special meeting.
The 36-seat Negeri Sembilan assembly was officially dissolved last Friday to pave the way for the election. — Bernama

Title: Pekanbaru–Melaka flights to boost health tourism from Indonesia, says exco
Article:
MELAKA, June 8 — The Pekanbaru-Melaka-Pekanbaru flight route, which will begin operating at Batu Berendam International Airport on Wednesday (June 10), is expected to invigorate the state’s health tourism sector through the arrival of visitors from Indonesia seeking medical treatment in Melaka.
State Works, Infrastructure, Public Facilities and Transportation Committee chairman Datuk Hameed Mytheen Kunju Basheer said the flight service, operated by Wings Air, will operate three times a week, namely Wednesdays, Fridays and Sundays.
“There is a high demand for health services in Melaka among Indonesians, and this route will make it easier for them to obtain treatment in the state.
“Previously, many used the ferry service, but now, with this flight route, they have an option to either travel by ferry or by air,” he told a media conference at the Batu Berendam International Airport here today.
Hameed said the state government, through the Tourism Promotion Division (BPP), will collaborate with private hospitals to intensify health tourism promotions to attract more visitors from Indonesia to Melaka.
He said the flight for the Pekanbaru-Melaka-Pekanbaru route is set to operate using the ATR 72 aircraft, which can carry 72 passengers.
The first flight is scheduled to begin on Wednesday, with more than 50 passengers expected to arrive from Pekanbaru at the Batu Berendam International Airport.
He said the flight is scheduled to arrive at 12.40pm, then return to Pekanbaru at 1.10pm.
Meanwhile, Hameed said another new route, namely Melaka to Penang, by Batik Air is expected to operate within the next two months.
He said the route has received approval from Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) and is currently undergoing the process related to the Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM).
“However, the dates and frequency of flights have yet to be finalised by the airline company,” he said.
He said the addition of the flight route was among the state government’s efforts to strengthen Melaka’s air transportation system, thus increasing the presence of tourists and investors from both within and outside the country. — Bernama

Title: Genting inheritance dispute: Lim Goh Tong’s daughter signed will in hospital, court told
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — The High Court hearing of the Genting Group family’s will dispute heard today that one of the contested wills was signed at a private hospital while the late Lim Siew Kim, daughter of Lim Goh Tong, was undergoing treatment for ovarian cancer.
Low Beng Choo, 68, a lawyer who prepared two wills for the estate of the late Siew Kim, said one of the wills was signed on April 11, 2022.
He further denied any suggestion that the physical and mental condition of the late Siew Kim, at the time she is alleged to have executed the second will, had impaired her capacity to understand or comprehend the nature and extent of the assets she was disposing of.
Low, the sole remaining trustee of Dikim Foundation, the main beneficiary of Siew Kim’s allegedly RM1 billion estate, testified under cross-examination by lawyer Datuk V. Sithambaram, counsel for two of the deceased’s daughters, Chan T’Shiao Li and Kimberly Chan T’Shiao Miin.
The sisters filed the suit in 2023 against four defendants, including Low and their brother Marcus Chan Jau Chwen, challenging their late mother’s mental capacity to execute the will and alleging suspicious circumstances surrounding its preparation.
Low, the fourth defendant, had drafted and witnessed Siew Kim’s wills dated April 11 and April 28, 2022, and had also acted as a witness to her earlier will dated November 2, 2021.
Sithambaram asked Low if she knew that the deceased was being treated for ovarian cancer, fluid in her lungs causing breathlessness, and other ailments when she was called to Prince Court Medical Centre on April 11, 2022. “I do not know fully,” Low responded.
Sithambaram: Given the physical and mental condition of the deceased, at the time the second will was allegedly executed, in fact, the deceased was not in the position to understand the extent of what she was executing.
Low:
I totally disagree.
Counsel further submitted that Siew Kim had not been assessed as cognitively fit by any qualified medical practitioner, including a psychiatrist or neurologist, before executing the second will. Low again disagreed with the suggestion.
Low informed the court that a doctor had witnessed the signing of the will. She stated that it was her general practice to request a doctor’s presence whenever she witnessed the execution of a will within a hospital setting.
Siew Kim, the third child of the Genting Group founder, succumbed to cancer in July 2022 at the age of 73. She is survived by three daughters and one son.
The hearing before Judge Mahazan Mat Taib resumes tomorrow. — Bernama

Title: High Court to rule Aug 5 on appeal over blogger’s acquittal in YouTube posts involving ex-PM Ismail Sabri, Umno
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 8— The High Court here today fixed August 5 to rule on the prosecution’s appeal against the Sessions Court’s acquittal of human rights blogger Jufazli Shi Ahmad.
The appeal stems from two charges of making offensive posts against former prime minister Datuk Seri Ismail Sabri Yaakob and Umno via YouTube, and failing to provide a YouTube account password to police officers.
Judge Datuk Aslam Zainuddin set the date after hearing submissions from deputy public prosecutor Mohamad Shahrizzat Amadan and defence counsel Ramesh N.P. Chandran, representing Jufazli, 37.
On October 18, 2024, the Sessions Court acquitted and discharged Jufazli on both charges without calling for his defence.
In his submissions today, Mohamad Shahrizzat contended that the Sessions Court judge had erred in ruling that no evidence linked the respondent to the uploaded video, and that this error justifies an intervention by the High Court.
Meanwhile, Ramesh said the prosecution failed to adduce any evidence whatsoever from YouTube to prove that the respondent was the administrator or owner of the account known as “KingMaker”.
On July 7, 2022, Jufazli pleaded not guilty to uploading a video titled “Panas!! Supermodel Kantoi Merompak RM77.3 billion” on YouTube King Maker Politik at 3pm on June 27, 2022.
The offensive videos were viewed at 3pm at the Criminal Investigation Department of the Dang Wang district police headquarters on July 1, 2022.
The charge, framed under Section 233 (1) (a) of the Communications and Multimedia Act (CMA) 1998, carries a maximum fine of RM50,000 or imprisonment for up to one year or both, and is also liable to a further fine of RM1,000 for each day the offence persists after conviction.
Jufazli also claimed trial to a charge of failing to provide the password to his YouTube King Maker Politik account, stored on his mobile phone, to police officers at the Dang Wangi police headquarters at 12.10pm, July 5, 2022.
The charge was brought under Section 249 of the CMA, which is punishable by a maximum fine of RM100,000 or imprisonment of up to two years or both, upon conviction. — Bernama
